mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply weakness, which compresses corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically flat during the top quartile of FEDFUNDS monthly increases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when inflation expectations are explicitly controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The quarterly GDP growth rate shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year sample, or the sign of the relationship reverses consistently across two consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100, it signals a supply-side bottleneck or recessionary output gap, which reduces earnings visibility and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO trades below 100, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and declining real personal savings indicates weakening household balance sheets, which suppresses consumption-driven corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of observed instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising consumer price indices erode real purchasing power and trigger anticipatory rate hikes, which compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically flat during months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% monthly threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
